whatItIs: Search engines keep cached copies and stale results that can still show your former name after the original page has changed or come down.
actions: After the source page is updated or removed, use Google's 'Remove outdated content' tool to refresh or drop the cached result.
Use Google's 'Results about you' tool for results that expose personal contact information.
Repeat with Bing's content-removal tool, then re-check in a few weeks — caches refresh on their own schedule.
disclaimer: Informational only. Removing a cached copy doesn't remove the source page — opt out or fix the source too.
